HMW-V08: Non-FM Job Detail (OH/EC/CS/CR)

What does the vendor job detail page look like for services that aren't FM Trucking? No driver, no route — just a single location, fees, and status progression.
Non-FM services: OH (Origin Handling), EC (Export Customs), CS (Cargo Submission), CR (Cargo Retrieval). These all happen at a single facility, not a route. There's no driver assignment (the vendor's staff at that facility handle it). The vendor views this page for status monitoring and fee reconciliation. For OH jobs, hub ops does bag scanning (ARR/PAL/DEP) via WeChat. For EC/CS/CR, the vendor updates status manually or via proof upload.

OH Job — Origin Handling (with Hub Ops Scanning)

Hub Ops Service
OH is the most complex non-FM service. Hub ops scans bags through three stages: Inbound (ARR), Processing (PAL), Outbound (DEP). The vendor dispatcher needs to see progress through these stages. The question: how do we show ARR/PAL/DEP progress without overdesigning it?

Answer: a simple inline progress row showing the three stages with counts. Not a timeline, not badges — just "Inbound: 24/24 · Processing: 18/24 · Outbound: 0/24".

State: In Progress — Hub Ops Processing

EC Job — Export Customs Clearance

Standard Service
EC is simpler than OH. No hub ops scanning stages. The customs broker processes the declaration, and the job goes Pending → In Progress → Completed (proof uploaded) → Verified. The vendor sees location, MAWB (important for customs), fees, and proof documents.

State: Completed — Awaiting Verification

CS Job — Cargo Submission

CS follows the same pattern as EC. Single location (airport terminal), standard status flow, fees, proof. The only difference is the fee names. The page layout is identical to EC.

State: Verified

Job created · Ops Admin

Non-FM Job Detail: One Layout, Service-Aware Content

All non-FM services share the same page layout: Status Bar → Location → [Hub Ops Progress for OH only] → Cargo → Fees → Proofs → Activity Log

Location replaces Route. Single facility name + zone. No origin → destination. No driver assignment.

OH gets one extra section: Hub Ops Progress. Three counters — Inbound, Processed, Outbound — with bag counts. "24/24 · 18/24 · 0/24". Green when done, blue when in progress, gray when waiting. This is the only difference between OH and EC/CS/CR. Same compact inline row as Cargo. No timeline, no badges.

EC/CS/CR are identical layouts. The only thing that changes is the service name, location, and fee names. One component, different data.

Fees-first still applies for non-FM. But Location is above Fees because it's tiny (one line) and the vendor needs to confirm they're looking at the right facility before reviewing fees.
